target: reduce_window max/min large-window dense f64/f32 pooling
lever: Replace the existing separable per-axis window scan with a monotonic-deque line pass for max/min. The dispatch gate is unchanged: no window dilation, dense float input/output, reduce_op in {max,min}, and win_total > 2*win_sum.
alien_primitive: Monotonic deque sliding-window extrema; algorithmic replacement from repeated per-window rescans to amortized O(input+output) per axis.

baseline.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR RCH_DAEMON_WAIT_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_SECS=240 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-rch-yax7q-baseline cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/maxpool_256x256_15x15 --quick --noplot

after.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR RCH_DAEMON_WAIT_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_SECS=240 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-rch-yax7q-after cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/maxpool_256x256_15x15 --quick --noplot

isomorphism.ordering: Output shape and row-major output order are unchanged. Each axis pass still emits one value for each original output coordinate before the next axis pass.
isomorphism.floating_point: Max/min are the only routed reductions. NaNs are tracked per 1D window and emit canonical f64::NAN exactly like jax_max_f64/jax_min_f64. Signed zero preference matches f64::max (+0) and f64::min (-0). There is no sum reassociation, FMA, mixed precision, or tolerance-based comparison.
isomorphism.rng: No RNG state or random primitive is touched.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: The reducer returns scalar extrema, not indices. Equal nonzero values have identical bits; signed-zero preference is explicitly preserved.
isomorphism.fallback_surface: Sum, small windows, dilated windows, non-float outputs, and non-dense inputs keep the previous paths.
